steps.title: Find the per-unit decay factor
steps.content:
When $w$ increases by 1, the exponent $2w$ increases by 2. The ratio of consecutive outputs is:
$$\frac{z(w+1)}{z(w)} = \frac{(0.819)^{2w+2}}{(0.819)^{2w}} = (0.819)^2$$
Each unit increase in $w$ multiplies $z(w)$ by $(0.819)^2$.

steps.title: Compute the decay factor
steps.content: $(0.819)^2 = 0.670761 \approx 0.671$. So $z(w)$ retains about $67.1\%$ of its value after each unit increase in $w$.

steps.title: Convert to a percent decrease
steps.content: If $z(w)$ retains $67.1\%$, it decreases by $100\% - 67.1\% = 32.9\%$. So $p \approx 32.9$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) 0.181: Far too small — this would mean the function barely decreases. Eliminated.
  • B) 0.819: This is the base of the original function, not the percent decrease. Eliminated.
  • C) 18.1: This would be $1 - 0.819 = 0.181 = 18.1\%$, the decrease per unit increase in $2w$, not in $w$. Eliminated.
  • D) 32.9: Matches the computed $100(1 - 0.819^2) \approx 32.9\%$. Correct.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
